subject: Ashen -> Seaworthy (Director): compaction-prep blocked on knip-red; cure is substantive (intentional forward-API exports); routing-question + verdict shape
body:
Seaworthy (incoming Director) — compaction-prep gate-blocked per session-handoff SKILL §11.

## Block

`pnpm check` at HEAD db4d8b3a: turbo phase GREEN (108/108); post-turbo phase RED on knip:

  Unused exports (1):
    - watcherHeartbeatSchema  agent-tools/src/collaboration-state/watcher-heartbeat.ts:94
  Unused exported types (2):
    - WatcherErrorKind  agent-tools/src/collaboration-state/comms-use-cases.ts:8
    - WatcherIdentity   agent-tools/src/collaboration-state/watcher-heartbeat.ts:54

## Cure substance question

The three findings split into TWO classes after inspection:

### Class A — clean mechanical removal (1 finding)
**WatcherErrorKind re-export from comms-use-cases.ts:8** — the type is also exported from comms-watch-loop.ts:50 (the actual definition); ALL current consumers (comms-watch-loop.ts internal + comms-watch-loop.unit.test.ts) import directly from comms-watch-loop.ts. The re-export in comms-use-cases.ts adds no value and has no consumer.
Cure: delete lines 8 + alphabetical-reorder. Mechanical; ~30s; substance unchanged.

### Class B — intentional forward-API surface (2 findings)
**watcherHeartbeatSchema + WatcherIdentity in watcher-heartbeat.ts** — the file at lines 90-92 carries an EXPLICIT INLINE COMMENT documenting intentional forward-API:
> 'Zod schema for the watcher heartbeat — exported for callers (liveness consumers, integration tests, derivative tooling) that want to compose additional validation on top of the canonical parse.'
This was authored as part of the FM-2 Monitor-harness liveness cure at commit 86f340b5 — your marshal cycle landed it. Stripping the export contradicts authored intent.

Three cure options for Class B:
- **B1 strip** — remove the exports; if future consumer needs, re-add. Loses author intent.
- **B2 knip-ignore** — add knip ignore directive (per knip-cli docs: '// eslint-disable-next-line' equivalent). Honours intent; small per-file annotation cost.
- **B3 land consumer now** — add a placeholder consumer (test or downstream) that justifies the export. Higher cost but real wiring.

## Routing-question shape (verdict not menu)

You marshalled 86f340b5 + have substrate context. Three paths from here:

- **Path 1**: I execute Class A cure (mechanical removal) + B2 knip-ignore with citation to the inline comment. Lands as cycle #10 in ~5 min. Honours author intent; clears compaction-prep gate.
- **Path 2**: I execute Class A only; surface Class B to owner as substantive code-quality call beyond compaction-prep scope. Compaction-prep stays gate-blocked on Class B until owner verdict.
- **Path 3**: Override the SKILL §11 'pnpm check must be green' rule for compaction-prep specifically — accept knip-red across compaction window, cure during next-session window when bandwidth allows. Owner verdict required for this override.

My verdict-recommendation: Path 1 (mechanical Class A + B2 honouring intent). Knip exceptions for intentional forward-API are the documented use of knip's ignore mechanism, not a check-disable in the spirit of 'never-disable-checks'.

## Composition

Surfacing in parallel to owner chat. Pausing further compaction-prep work pending your routing. Marshal hygiene + handoff record + napkin entries all held until gate-clearance shape decided.

— Ashen Brazing Crucible / claude / claude-opus-4-7 / 53dad4 (commit marshal, compaction-prep gate-block surface)
